observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the ICO - Municipal Treasurer (i) to deposit intact all collections remitted to her by the collecting officers; (ii) to require all the collectors to remit to her all their collections for the day and prepare the RCD at the close of the business day or on the next working day; (iii) not to issue new sets of accountable forms until all collections were remitted in full by the collectors; (iv )strengthen the internal control in cash management by observing the proper cut-off in the reporting of collections, particularly the preparation of RCD; (v) to instruct all the collecting officers to (1) ensure completeness and accuracy of information on the accountable forms and RCDs such as signatures and dates; and (2) prepare and submit the monthly Report on Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF); (vi) to prepare and submit the monthly Consolidated Report on Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; (vii) to demand the collecting officers for the immediate remittance of all collections from previous months which were not yet turned over to her, if there are any; and (viii) not to collect additional fees for Livestock Development Fund; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors mentioned above and always be guided by the description of accounts provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 in recording the transactions of the Municipality.

In view of the above observations, we recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Engineer to (i) properly prepare/review with extra care the documents prepared/originated from that office  to avoid errors which could mislead readers and users of the documents; (ii) ensure that variation/change orders are supported with the necessary documents as required under Item 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2009-001;and (iii) be guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity of PPAs of the Municipality; and
Municipal Accountant to (i) be guided by the requirements of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 474(b)(5) of R.A. No. 7160 for proper documentation of every claim against government funds; (ii) properly record each transaction of the Municipality in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (iii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the incorrect recording mentioned above.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to:
ensure the consistency of information provided in various documents;
conduct the post-qualification process properly, if necessary, conduct further investigation to validate the claims of the bidder, and
ensure compliance with the requirements and procedures prescribed under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to prevent the same lapses in future procurements.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all inventories and PPEs of the Municipality and submit the corresponding report to the Audit Team; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements and ensure the completeness of supporting documents before making adjustments.

In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.  Also, require the Head of the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize monthly depreciation expenses to properly reflect the correct carrying amount of the PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all Inventories and PPE of the Municipality and submit the corresponding report to the Audit Team; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements and ensure the completeness of supporting documents before making adjustments.
In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.  Also, require the Head of the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and reconcile the balances of the accounts affected by the transfer and use of LDRRM Fund.  Moreover, disclose properly the balances of the Trust Liability – DRRM in the Notes to Financial Statements.
We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer incorporate the programs, projects and activities to be charged from the unexpended balances of the previous years’ LDRRMF in the LDRRMFIP.  Also, ensure that minimum requirements of equipage and pre-positioned food packs and hygiene kits are in place so that the Municipality could timely and efficiently respond in times of calamities.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
the Local Finance Committee identify additional sources of income;
the Municipal Treasurer (i) determine doable measures to improve collection efficiency of internally sourced taxes, fees and charges; (ii) keep a master list of stallholders and indicate therein the vital information such as stall/table number, description of location whether it is  inside, outside or corner stall, stall area, rate per square meter or rate per day; and (iii) maintain a subsidiary ledger of each stallholder; and
the Local Chief Executive execute contract of lease with the seven stallholders.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the Municipal Planning and Development Council to identify priority development projects, review existing projects whether its implementation is compliant with the guidelines set and ensure that PPAs to be charged against the 20% DF includes only those allowed under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and
the Municipal Engineer to ensure timely, efficient and effective implementation of development projects so that economic benefits that could be derived therefrom will be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GFPS and its TWG to ensure and sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues, and take a lead role in direction-setting, advocacy, planning, monitoring and evaluation, and technical advisory in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in the LGU programs, projects, activities, and processes.
We also recommended the GFPS and its TWG (a) to ensure that at least 5% of the LGU’s total budget be allocated to GAD related PPAs to achieve the country’s commitment, plans and policies in GAD, gender equality and women’s empowerment; and (b) furnish the Audit Team with the DILG-endorsed GAD Plan and Budget to facilitate better assessment and understanding of the Municipality’s Gender and Development programs.
We further recommended that the Implementing Offices ensure effective and efficient implementation of GAD-related PPAs so that benefits that could be derived therefrom will be enjoyed by the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
give preferential attention on the submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006, COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 70, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs;
revert back to proper cash in bank and payable accounts all stale checks; and
submit the BRS for all of the Municipality’s bank accounts within the reglementary period.

observations.reason: There was no action taken by Management to fully recognize depreciation on prior years' PPE due to unavailability of accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the BAC Secretariat: a. to submit to the Auditor copies of approved purchase orders and their supporting documents within the reglementary period of five (5) days after perfection/issuance; and b. to comply strictly with COA Circulars 95-006, 89-299A and 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: There was no action taken by Management to fully comply with the submission timelines.
observations.reason: The same observation was noted in CY 2017 audit. Copies of POs and supporting documents were still not furnished within the reglementary period as required.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer remit the excess and unutilized PDAF funds to the Bureau of Treasury and ask for Official Receipt for the same pursuant to Supreme Court decisions declaring PDAF acts and practices unconstitutional and in compliance to Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was directed to remit the unutilized PDAF funds to the Bureau of Treasury and request for Official Receipt for the same.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant could not verify whether the unutilized balance were indeed remitted to the Bureau of Treasury due to unavailability of records to support the balance.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to (i) ensure timely deposit of collections received from the collectors; (ii) instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to her; (iii) ensure correctness of figures presented on the face of RCD and as much as possible avoid erasures; (iv) instruct the collecting officers to ensure that Official Receipts are issued in chronological sequence; (v) refrain from receiving incomplete remittances from the collecting officers; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the amounts of recorded transactions and adjusts the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections.
The ICO – Municipal Treasurer took note of the recommendations and promised compliance thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and submit the corresponding report to the Audit Team; and
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.
In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made. Also, require the Head of the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the ICO - Municipal Treasurer to make payment of the loan amortization on or before the due date to avoid default and payment of penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure written approval from the Governor for any official travel outside the province in accordance with Section 96 of R.A. 7160.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer stop the charging of prior years’ expenditures against current year appropriation.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant scrutinize the supporting documents before making payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRM Council to formulate a comprehensive Local Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan of the Municipality to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the disaster risk management activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to:
1. furnish the Audit Team with copies of POs and their supporting documents within five (5) days after issuance thereof to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same, so that the Management could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon;
2. scrutinize the documents submitted by the bidder and exercise diligence in the post-qualification; and
3. properly evaluate the mode of procurement to be used and strictly adhere with the provision set forth by the procurement law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the MDRRM Council to review, evaluate and cause the implementation of projects under the continuing appropriation so that the constituents could enjoy the benefits that could be derived from this PPAs and refrain from charging maintenance and other operating expenses of the LDRRMO against the LDRRMF;
b. the Municipal Treasurer to immediately issue the OR upon the receipt of fund and prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits so that the Accounting Office could record the transactions; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 and reconcile the balances of the accounts affected by the transfer and use of LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly and uniformly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to require all officers and employees who are still in the service to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending demand letters; c) in case of failure to settle/liquidate their cash advances, cause or order the withholding of the payment of their salaries as provided for under Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and impose the sanctions provided under the penal provision of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 and/or study the possibility of filing the appropriate charges to enforce settlement thereof in accordance with Section 9.3.3 of the same Circular and Article 217 and 218 of the Revised Penal Code; d) direct the concerned personnel to strictly require an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities; e) instruct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless prior cash advance given was first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; and f) require the Municipal Treasurer to report intact the collections and immediately prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits so that the Accounting Office could henceforth record the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant imposed salary deduction as settlement for the unliquidated cash advances of prior years. Demand letters were also sent to accountable officers including those who are no longer in service. The Municipal Mayor requested for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances of Mr. Ramsie Lunasco, deceased Planning and Development Coordinator, totaling P79,353.75; the request was granted by the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, 87.91% of the unliquidated cash advances pertains to those granted in prior years. There was no action taken by Management on some accounts. Some unliquidated cash advances remained dormant and unresolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) adhere with the accounting policy of recording in the Trust Fund Books funds transferred to the Municipality; and b) direct the officers concerned to fast tract the implementation of the projects funded by NGAs and cause the immediate liquidation of the funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already transferred the completed projects to Government Equity at year-end. Also, we transferred the unliquidated balance of the projects to the General Fund. The Municipal Mayor direct the officers concerned to properly monitor and implement the projects funded by NGAs to ensure timely completion thereof so that maximum benefits derived therefrom will be enjoyed by the constituents.
observations.reason: Verification of the Subsidiary Ledgers as of year-end revealed a year-end balance amounting to P1,797,512.35 and P475,775.34 of which pertains to the unliquidated balance of the projects while the amount of P1,321,767.01 pertains to the unreconciled balance of the projects, which remained unsettled due to unavailability of documents. Delays were still noted in the implementation of projects totaling P1,289,290.00 funded by NGAs. Lapses were noted in procurement of three projects in violation of RA No. 9184. The Municipal Accountant already remitted the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH contributions and amortizations to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH. Unremitted and unreconciled amounts remained due to lack of proper documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH and make the necessary adjustments and instruct the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees whose GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH contributions and loan amortizations are unremitted and make the necessary adjustments and effect the immediate remittance of unrecorded remittances prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor already remitted the Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH contributions and amortizations to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH; and make the necessary adjustments and instruct the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees whose GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PHILHEALTH contributions and loan amortizations are unremitted.
observations.reason: Due to lack of proper documentation, some remittances remained unreconciled and unremitted. These also pertains to the amounts prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Sangguniang Bayan Members to exercise prudence in spending government funds by limiting the number of attendees in conventions and conferences. It is suggested that only members of the SB committee directly concerned with the subject of the conference should attend.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the SB Members limited their attendance to PCL-sponsored seminars and trainings. It is only during the PCL Convention which was held once a year that all or almost all members of the SB attended the conference called for.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman to: a) consider the provisions of R.A. 9184 with regard to submission of late bids; b) invite the other two Observers described in Section 13.1 of R.A. No. 9184 in succeeding procurement activities; c) submit the notice issued to the bidder with the Single Calculated Bid; d) conduct a comprehensive post qualification; and e) post the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed within the prescribed period for posting in the PhilGEPS.
observations.managementAction: There was no action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Late bids were still submitted in the Bids during the year. Deficiencies were still noted in the issuance of notice to bidder with LCB as well as in the post-qualification process conducted by the BAC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) direct the Local Development Council to appraise the socio-economic and environmental conditions of the locality to identify the programs and projects to be given priority; b) instruct the MPDC to properly monitor the implementation of the projects; and c) direct the officials in-charge to submit the Municipal Development Council Resolution for the reprogrammed projects as well as their corresponding appropriations. Also, submit the Memorandum of Agreement for projects which the National Government have their counterpart. We also recommended that subsidiary ledgers for each development projects be maintained to facilitate proper monitoring of programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund; and the BAC ensure that all procurement shall be in accordance with the approved APP.
observations.managementAction: The projects during CY 2017 were implemented in CY 2018. The Municipal Mayor instructed the Local Development Council to identify the PPAs to be given priority and follow the Joint Memorandum Circular in the implementation of projects. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) was instructed to properly monitor the implementation of the projects and submit the Municipal Development Council Resolution for reprogrammed projects. The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for each development projects. The BAC to adhere to procurement provisions.
observations.reason: Lapses were still noted in the procurement of various projects funded under the 20% DF. Lapses were still noted in the disbursement of the 20% Development Fund and deficiencies remained in procurement processes; some projects were implemented in CY2018 although planned earlier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Office/MDRRMO to formulate, promote and implement a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan that supports disaster risk management activities and be incorporated in the local development plan and financial plan of the Municipality. Also, adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 and ensure compliance with procurement laws and timely submission of contracts and POs to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO prepared the LDRRMFIP for CY 2018. The Municipal Mayor issued an Executive Order strengthening the MDRRMC of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and transferred the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund account at year-end. Municipal Mayor issued an Executive Order No. 029 S.2016 strengthening the MDRRMC of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Deficiencies were still noted in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP, on recording and utilization of the LDDRMF. Lapses were noted in the procurement of goods through alternative mode totaling P2,233,643.50 in violation of RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR; unspent continuing appropriations remained unimplemented and documentation and transfer entries required reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the GAD Focal Person to ensure that at least 5% of the Municipality’s total budgets be allocated to GAD-related PPAs; b) perform HGDG Assessment on PPAs which are regular/flagship programs of the Municipality to determine the amount attributable to GAD; c) be more specific in the PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget; d) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents before making payments; and e) BAC to strictly adhere with procurement rules.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person conducted meeting with the GFPS Members to timely furnish the GAD Plan and Budget and its Accomplishment Report. The Municipal Accountant ensured completeness before payments. BAC to strictly adhere to procurement process.
observations.reason: The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018 was endorsed by the DILG. The GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 was submitted on March 8, 2019 upon written request by the Audit Team due to the failure of implementing offices to submit the reports on time to the GAD Focal Person. Allocation for GAD still fell short by P221,152.35. Deficiencies were still noted in procurement processes of projects funded out of the GAD Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds and identify the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made reciprocal adjustments reducing the balance of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds account to P225,574.90 and P737,497.38, respectively, and Due to Other Funds account to P1,248,038.59.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, the reciprocal accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds account to P2,039,423.49 remained unreconciled. Some balances remained unsettled due to unavailability of supporting documents and pending further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: f) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly and uniformly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002; g) instruct the Municipal Accountant to require all officers and employees who are still in the service to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification or demand letters; h) in case of failure to settle/liquidate their cash advances, cause or order the withholding of the payment of their salaries as provided for under Section 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and impose the sanctions provided under the penal provision of Section 128 of PD No. 1445 and/or study the possibility of filing the appropriate charges to enforce settlement thereof in accordance with Section 9.3.3 of the same Circular and Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code; i) require strictly an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Vol. I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled; j) refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless prior cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and k) make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the understatement of Advances to Officers and Employees (Account 148) and the related accounts affected.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant imposed salary deduction as settlement for the unliquidated cash advances of prior years. Demand letters were also sent to accountable officers including those who are no longer in service. The Municipal Mayor requested for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances of Mr. Ramsie Lunasco, deceased Planning and Development Coordinator, totaling P79,353.75; the request was granted by the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, 87.91% of the unliquidated cash advances pertains to those granted in prior years. The Municipal Accountant requested for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances of Mr. Ramsie Lunasco, deceased Planning and Development Coordinator totaling P79,353.75; the request was granted by the Commission on Audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) comply strictly with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 and submit the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for approval; b) ensure closure of open dumpsite and operation of sanitary landfill; and c) implement proper waste management and disposal measures.
observations.managementAction: The open dumpsite was closed and the approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was prepared. The Municipal Mayor issued actions to comply and strengthened the MDRRMC and SWM activities.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWM Plan was approved. However, the Municipality is still operating a controlled dumpsite as a transitory waste facility pending establishment of a sanitary landfill. Some households still practice open burning in remote areas.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a) stop the practice of payment of expenses through reimbursement; b) comply strictly with the guidelines that payments by checks should be in the name of the supplier so that applicable taxes may be deducted from the purchases made; and c) adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the adoption of shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant limited the payment of expenses through reimbursement and instructed staff to comply with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) the LDRRMO or the employee concerned to submit the LDRRMFIP for CY 2015 and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; b) the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF and adhere strictly with COA Circular No. 2012-002; and c) the Municipal Mayor to establish the LDRRMO and strengthen the MDRRMC to comply with RA No. 10121 and relevant joint memoranda.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 029 S.2016 strengthening the MDRRMC of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF. The LDRRMO prepared the LDRRMFIP for CY 2018.
observations.reason: Deficiencies were still noted in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP and in recording and utilization of the LDRRMF. The unexpended balance was disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements as of December 31, 2017, but lapses in procurement documentation and adherence to procedures remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, should see to it that all the necessary documentary requirement are attached to the vouchers and that the disbursements for infrastructure projects be strictly discussed and supported before processing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, as internal auditor, checked the documentary requirements and ensured the necessary documents be submitted before processing disbursements. Officials were reminded to comply.
observations.reason: Verification of the payments made for CY 2018 revealed that there were still documentary requirements that were not attached in the disbursement vouchers. Some supporting documents were still lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Council to identify priority development projects that constitute the nature of capital expenditures; b) the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate account as prescribed by Section 313 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 as well as the necessary subsidiary ledgers pertaining thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and concerned officials complied with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular in regard to projects to be implemented. The Municipal Mayor instructed the MPDC to identify the priority PPAs and Municipal Accountant to maintain separate accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) the Municipal Mayor ensure adequate allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for priority programs and projects that address gender issues or women’s concerns; b) the GAD Focal Point System members ensure that PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan constitute gender issues and ensure proper implementation and monitoring thereof; and c) the concerned officials prepare reliable reports and be reminded of the deadline of submission of various GAD-related reports to different monitoring agencies.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018 was endorsed by the DILG. GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 was submitted on March 8, 2019 upon written request by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The GAD appropriation for CY 2018 was endorsed by the DILG; nevertheless, the appropriation for GAD still fell short by P221,152.35. Some implementing offices failed to submit required reports on time; deficiencies still noted in procurement funded by GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CYs 2008 to 2014 for the LGU’s bank accounts under the Trust Fund (except for ARCP2 LGU Equity and Loan Proceeds) and for CYs 2009 to 2014 for the LGU’s bank accounts for General and Special Education Funds and submit same to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification, as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account. Necessary adjustments, if any, should be effected at once to avoid accumulation thereof and to present the correct balance of the cash in bank account in the financial statements. Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was advised to give priority to the timely submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the said report to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Delays were still noted in the submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements and the BRS for CY 2018 was prepared and submitted but prior years' delays remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: 1) instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to the OIC – Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 29, Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual; 2) be more circumspect in the acknowledgement of remittances in terms of accuracy of the amount; 3) ensure completeness of information on the face of RCD such as date and number of the report, the date of verification, acknowledgement and signature of the signatories; 4) comply with the prescribed format of RCD in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and that the Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the amounts of the recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the errors in computations, as appropriate and adjust the Cash in Bank account as appropriate to reflect the correct balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer instructed collectors to prepare daily RCDs and remit all collections; Municipal Accountant to ensure accuracy of recorded transactions and adjust balances as appropriate.
observations.reason: Report of Collections and Deposits were not prepared daily by the collecting officers. This was discussed in Finding No. 2 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly and uniformly implement the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and EO provisions; b) require the Municipal Accountant to verify details of account balance and require all officers and employees who are still in service to settle/liquidate all their outstanding cash advances by sending confirmation, notification or demand letters; c) in case of failure to settle/liquidate cause or order the withholding of the payment of their salaries as provided; d) require Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities; e) refrain from granting additional cash advances unless prior is settled; and f) make necessary adjusting entries to correct understatement of Advances to Officers and Employees and related accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant imposed salary deduction as settlement for unliquidated cash advances. Demand letters were sent; the Municipal Mayor requested authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances of Mr. Ramsie Lunasco; request granted by COA.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018, 87.91% of the unliquidated cash advances pertains to those granted in prior years. Some accounts remain unresolved. Some amounts were reclassified and adjusted; lack of documents caused delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from and Due to Other Funds by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding ledgers and exert extra effort to secure pertinent documents that will support the adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made reciprocal adjustments reducing the balance of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds account and identified causes of discrepancies.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2018 the reciprocal accounts still had unreconciled amounts (P2,039,423.49) and some balances remained unsettled as of December 31, 2017 and 2018; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 27.1 the LDRRMO or the employee concerned to submit the LDRRMFIP for CY 2015 and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B to the Audit Team; 27.2 the Municipal Accountant to disclose the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement and adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and 27.3 the Municipal Mayor to establish the LDRRMO and strengthen the MDRRMC which shall be responsible for setting the direction, development, implementation and coordination of disaster risk management programs within the jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 029 S.2016 strengthening the MDRRMC of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant disclosed the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements. The LDRRMO prepared the LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 and the LDRRM Office was organized.
observations.reason: The LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 was prepared. The unexpended balance was disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements as of December 31, 2017. However, deficiencies persisted in procurement and documentation; the comprehensive and properly documented LDRRMFIP and full compliance remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to observe strictly the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 specifically in the procurement of infrastructure projects to ensure adherence to transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring and to properly address the above noted lapses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor and BAC were reminded to adhere to procurement rules and ensure adherence to the principles of transparency and competitiveness. The BAC committed to comply.
observations.reason: Deficiencies in procurement remained and were again noted in CY 2017 and CY 2018 audits. Several procurement lapses were reiterated in Finding Nos. 6, 7, 8 and 10 of the 2018 report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to be cautious in the use of different alternative modes of procurement allowed by R.A. No. 9184. Ensure that the conditions and required documents for each alternative mode are first met. Likewise, remind the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, to adhere to the basic documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was reminded and instructed to strictly adhere to the prescribed procedures for alternative methods of procurement and to ensure documentary requirements are complied with.
observations.reason: Various deficiencies in procurement persisted and were still noted in subsequent audits; some corrective actions implemented but lapses remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Section 70, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was advised to give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports and to submit same within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: Delays were still noted in CY 2018 audit for submission of accounts and financial reports; some improvement but prior years' delays remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, should ensure that all documentary requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claim.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, as internal auditor, checked the documentary requirements and instructed the requisitioning and implementing offices to submit complete supporting documents prior to payment processing.
observations.reason: Still noted that some documentary requirements were not attached to disbursement vouchers; improvements were made but not all issues resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) ensure adequate allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for priority programs and projects that address gender issues or women’s concerns and submit the plan to the appropriate offices and secure the certificate of approval from the DILG Regional Office; and b) direct the GAD Focal Point Person to prepare reliable reports and be reminded of the deadline of submission of various GAD-related reports to different monitoring agencies.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018 was endorsed by the DILG and the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 was submitted on March 8, 2019 upon written request by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The GAD appropriation for CY 2018 was endorsed by DILG but appropriation still fell short by P221,152.35. Some implementing offices failed to submit reports on time; documentation deficiencies remained.

observations.recommendation:

the Municipal Mayor ensure adequate allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for priority projects, programs and activities that address gender issues or women’s concerns; and
the GAD Focal Point System members ensure that PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget constitute gender issues and ensure proper implementation and monitoring thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

 require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team; and

 thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count of Inventories with the accounting records in order to reflect the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.

In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.  Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the IIRUP which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted to the Audit Team the Report on the Physical Count of Properties, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for CY 2016.
observations.reason:
Analysis of the submitted RPCPPE and RPCI vis-à-vis accounting records revealed unreconciled differences amounting to P6,620,682.33 and P486,103.75, respectively.  Moreover, the  Audit Team observed that no inventory reports were submitted on some properties of the  agency.  

This is reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CYs 2008 to 2014 for the LGU’s bank accounts under the Trust Fund (except for ARCP2 LGU Equity and Loan Proceeds) and for CYs 2009 to 2014 for the LGU’s bank accounts for General and Special Education Funds and submit same to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification, as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 in order to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.  Necessary adjustments, if any, should be effected at once to avoid accumulation thereof and to present the correct balance of the cash in bank account in the financial statements.  Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the monthly bank reconciliation statements covering the period January 2013 to December 2016 on the PNB account maintained by the LGU for the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Late receipt of the bank statements is one of the reasons that caused the delay in the submission of monthly bank reconciliation statements for CY 2016 as cited by the Municipal Accountant during the exit conference.

observations.managementAction: Reclassification was already made on April 2016 by the Municipal Accountant for the unliquidated cash advances of Ms. Estrella Maño amounting to P11,710.00.  Also, the Municipal Accountant already made adjusting entries on the same month for the understatement totaling P4,518.00 of the account Advances to Officers and Employees and the related accounts affected.
observations.reason: Cash Advances as of December 31, 2016 amounting to P5,931,432.45 remained unliquidated.  The huge balance of the unliquidated cash advances mostly pertains to previous years for which the Municipality failed to pursue settlement through liquidation or refund.  All the cash advances for payroll granted during the year were liquidated as of year-end.  As to the erroneous recording of liquidation in the previous year amounting to P2,300.00 in the General Fund instead of the Trust Fund, the Municipal Accountant already made a correcting entry in the General Fund in CY 2016, however, no corresponding entry was made in the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRMO or the employee concerned to submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B to the Audit Team, in compliance with Items 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 respectively of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  Maintain also a separate subsidiary ledger for DRRM activities in the Trust Fund books.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the establishment of LDRRM Office which shall be responsible for setting the direction, development, implementation and coordination of disaster risk management programs within the jurisdiction.  Likewise, adhere strictly to Republic Act No. 10121 and NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2016.  The Municipal Mayor, through the Executive Order No. 029 s. 2016, created the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Council composed of other department heads who shall be in call in times of calamities.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements the unexpended/unutilized balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2016.  However, upon verification, it was observed that the balance of the 70% Mitigation Fun d for CY 2016 was not apportioned whether Capital Outlay or MOOE.  Furthermore, the Municipality still failed to comply with the composition and structure of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.  The LDRRMO commented during the exit conference that the Municipality cannot fill up the two (2) vacant positions because of the PS limitation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and furnish the Provincial Accountant with a Summary of JEVs showing the breakdown of the amounts Due to LGUs.  We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant make an adjustment to correct the overstated balance of RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding deferred accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made an adjustment to correct the overstated balance of RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding deferred accounts.  The Municipal Assessor commented during the exit conference that she furnished the Municipal Treasurer with the latest valuations of property of each taxpayer.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list of Real Property Taxpayers showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.  Hence, the Municipal Accountant has no basis in setting up Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant was advised to give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Section 70, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Management exerted effort in minimizing the delay incurred in the submission of accounts, monthly trial balances and quarterly financial reports.
observations.reason: Delay noted in the submission of accounts, monthly trial balances and quarterly financial reports for CY 2016 by the Accounting Office were as long as 118,108 and 63 days, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow the provisions of DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 in identifying the programs/projects/activities to be funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation.  See to it also that those development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.  Avoid charging expenses that are not related to the projects identified.  Lastly, submit LGU Accomplishment Report showing the status of implementation of the identified PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The Management already submitted Accomplishment Report for CY 2016.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to fully utilize the 20% Development Fund for CY 2016.
